risk_if_not_implemented: Without User-Initiated Logouts / Message Displays, security events may go undetected, allowing threats to persist unnoticed and increasing breach dwell time.

compensating_control_1.name: Re-Authentication
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to force users and devices to re-authenticate according to organization-defined circumstances that necessitate re-authentication.
compensating_control_1.justification: Re-Authentication (IAC-14) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of User-Initiated Logouts / Message Displays (IAC-25.1) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Session Lock
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to initiate a session lock after an organization-defined time period of inactivity, or upon receiving a request from a user and retain the session lock until the user reestablishes access using established identification and authentication methods.
compensating_control_2.justification: Session Lock (IAC-24) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of User-Initiated Logouts / Message Displays (IAC-25.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
